results.description: Airlock or external door (Not just applicable to space stations but also to settlements where the external environment is hostile)
Botanical garden (small neighbourhood park, empty plot of land)
Casino (high speed race course, arena fighting, back-room poker game, pachinko parlour, pokies machine)
Club (night club, body-mod cult, hunter's bar with the heads of local fauna displayed on the walls)

Communication hub (weave/lan cafe, interface forlocal news feeds, official government-run communication array, satellite control centre owned by the rich and powerful, hidden/cobbled-together computer parts linked to a rebel network, gossipmongers hanging around the water recyclers)
Courier offices (local oro -planet couriers, dri s/passage maps trader)
Education (primary school, secondary college, university, trade school, military training, library, research facilities, dorm rooms)
Entertainment - VR feeds, illegal fighting ring (watching or participating),   racing  (on  land   or  in space, watching or participating)
Faction house (guild house, faction's satellite office)
Fence (for illegal goods or contraband eg. precursor artefact)
Gang territory (drug dealer's corner, organised crime lord's headquarters, local mercenary's hangout)
High society (expensive parties, elegant soirees, rich merchant's mansion)
Hotels (capsule hotel, hourly motels, shore leave dorms, leisure apartments)
Living quarters (apartments, houses on stilts, boathouses)
Military (training grounds, weapons depot, shipyard, research   facility,  command  centre, armoury)
Monorail station (subway station, intra-city public transport station)
Non-human specialist (for all your companion asset needs)
Offices (government offices, licensing offices, dock master's office, local governor's office, sector's political hub, skyrises
Red light district (seedyback alleys with shi ing shadows, strip clubs, high end entertainment establishments, full-emersion VR experiences, dodgy hourly motels)
Relaxation (massage parlour, spa, drug den, beauty parlour, see red light district)
Religious establishments (temple, church, mosque, glade in the light of the planet's two moons, a dark cavern in the depths of an precursor vault, embalmer)
Research facility (bioweapon, medical, educational, precursor artefact, exobiology)

Retail/market (shopping mall, computer parts market, clothing boutique, second-hand bio hack, fake precursor tech, local food market, black iron trader, street vendors, gadgets/trinket stalls, information brokers)
Sewers (underground passages, conduit in  a  space  station  filled with cables and wiring, smuggling routes)
Showroom (land/sea support vehicle, latest precursor artefact inspired technology)
Sporting facility (arena to fight the local wildlife, rock climbing on planets filled with canyons, anti-gravity basketball)
Traffic control centre (command centre on a space station, tra c control towerlocated on the outskirts of the city, frantic logistics hub in a merchant city)
